perturbation.original_text: Each Party recognizes that, except as expressly provided in any binding written agreement between the Parties that is executed on or after the Effective Date: (i) the other Party and its Representatives will be free to negotiate with, and to enter into any agreement or transaction with, any other interested party; and (ii) such Party will not have any rights or claims against the other Party or any of the other Party’s Representatives arising out of or relating to any transaction or proposed transaction involving the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party recognizes that, except as expressly provided in any binding written agreement between the Parties that is executed on or after the Effective Date: (i) the other Party and its Representatives will be free to negotiate with, and to enter into any agreement or transaction with, any other interested party; and (ii) such Party will have rights or claims against the other Party or any of the other Party’s Representatives arising out of or relating to any transaction or proposed transaction involving the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing 'will not have' to 'will have' in subsection (ii). This directly contradicts the preceding statement about freedom to negotiate, suggesting that one party *can* have claims against the other related to transactions, creating uncertainty and ambiguious enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party recognizes that, except as expressly provided in any binding written agreement between the Parties that is executed on or after the Effective Date: (i) the other Party and its Representatives will be free to negotiate with, and to enter into any agreement or transaction with, any other interested party; and (ii) such Party will have rights or claims against the other Party or any of the other Party’s Representatives arising out of or relating to any transaction or proposed transaction involving the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section (ii) from 'will not have any rights or claims' to 'will have rights or claims'. This directly contradicts the first part of the same section which states both parties are free to negotiate, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding whether a party can bring claims against the other, thus triggering the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, (a) if a court of competent jurisdiction decides that there is a prevailing Party in such litigation, then the non-prevailing Party will pay to the prevailing Party the reasonable legal fees incurred by the prevailing Party in connection with such litigation (including any appeal relating thereto) or (b) if a court of competent jurisdiction (i) does not opine on whether there is a prevailing Party in such litigation and (ii) determines that either Party or any of its Representatives has breached this Agreement, such breaching Party will be liable for, and will pay to the other Party, the reasonable legal fees incurred by the other Party in connection with such litigation (including any appeal relating thereto).
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, (a) if a court of competent jurisdiction decides that there is a prevailing Party in such litigation, then the prevailing Party will pay to the non-prevailing Party the reasonable legal fees incurred by the non-prevailing Party in connection with such litigation (including any appeal relating thereto) or (b) if a court of competent jurisdiction (i) does not opine on whether there is a prevailing Party in such litigation and (ii) determines that either Party or any of its Representatives has breached this Agreement, such breaching Party will be liable for, and will pay to the other Party, the reasonable legal fees incurred by the other Party in connection with such litigation (including any appeal relating thereto).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by switching the payment obligation in (a) so that the *prevailing* party pays the legal fees of the *non-prevailing* party. This is in direct conflict with the standard legal principle and creates ambiguity in the enforcement of legal fee responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, (a) if a court of competent jurisdiction decides that there is a prevailing Party in such litigation, then the prevailing Party will pay to the non-prevailing Party the reasonable legal fees incurred by the non-prevailing Party in connection with such litigation (including any appeal relating thereto) or (b) if a court of competent jurisdiction (i) does not opine on whether there is a prevailing Party in such litigation and (ii) determines that either Party or any of its Representatives has breached this Agreement, such breaching Party will be liable for, and will pay to the other Party, the reasonable legal fees incurred by the other Party in connection with such litigation (including any appeal relating thereto).
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by stipulating that the prevailing party pays the non-prevailing party's legal fees, which is the opposite of the standard legal principle and the original agreement. This creates a strong contradiction regarding the responsibility for legal fees in case of litigation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each Party and its Representatives and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each Party and its Representatives but not their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by excluding successors and assigns from benefiting. This contradicts the standard legal understanding that agreements generally transfer to successors and assigns, creating uncertainty about the agreement's scope of protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each Party and its Representatives but not their respective successors and assigns. This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware (without giving effect to principles of conflicts of laws). Each Party: (a) irrevocably and unconditionally consents and submits to the jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in the State of Delaware for purposes of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement; (b) agrees that service of any process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to the address set forth opposite the name of such Party at the end of this Agreement shall be effective service of process for any such action, suit or proceeding brought against such Party; (c) irrevocably and unconditionally waives any objection to the laying of venue of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement in any state or federal court located in the State of Delaware; and (d) irrevocably and unconditionally waives the right to plead or claim, and irrevocably and unconditionally agrees not to plead or claim, that any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement that is brought in any state or federal court located in the State of Delaware has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement would benefit successors and assigns. The changed text explicitly excludes successors and assigns from benefiting. The contradicted text repeats this exclusion. Since the original text allowed successors and assigns to benefit, and the changed text removes that benefit, a direct contradiction exists. The explanation also states the change introduces an in-text contradiction by excluding successors and assigns from benefiting, thereby creating uncertainty.
